payload.query: You just got a call from a well known research university that just established a Ph.D. degree in Political Science. As part of your evaluation, you are to submit a syllabus/report on what you would teach in a Comparative Politics Proseminar taken by all first-year Ph.D. students. Prepare a report that discusses the 6 most important topics in Comparative Politics that you will cover. For each topic discuss: a) why this is an important topic/why you chose it, b) most important contributors to the topic, c) state of knowledge on the topic, d) what needs to be done to advance the state of knowledge.

articles.title: Wars Often Lose Public Support Over Time. Trump Started This One Without Much.
articles.abstract: In opening a military campaign against Iran, President Trump is the first president in modern times to take the United States to war without the backing of the public.
articles.snippet: In opening a military campaign against Iran, President Trump is the first president in modern times to take the United States to war without the backing of the public.
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

articles.title: Pardon Industry Offers Rich Offenders a Path to Trump
articles.abstract: One inmate paid lobbyists and lawyers with ties to the president’s team and walked free. Others are following his blueprint, but it is not always clear who can deliver.
articles.snippet: One inmate paid lobbyists and lawyers with ties to the president’s team and walked free. Others are following his blueprint, but it is not always clear who can deliver.

articles.title: Trump Demands Iran’s ‘Unconditional Surrender’ as Israel Pounds Tehran and Lebanon
articles.abstract: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.
articles.snippet: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.
articles.byline: By David E. Sanger, Adam Rasgon, Euan Ward and Richard Pérez-Peña
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

articles.title: Trump Tightens Pressure on Cuba as U.S. Pushes for Charges Against Leaders
articles.abstract: The order could set the stage for a drive for regime change in Cuba, a goal President Trump has expressed.

articles.title: Robotaxis Put on Hold as Hochul Seeks Support for Car Insurance Reforms
articles.abstract: How two seemingly unrelated policies, one to reduce car insurance costs, the other to allow driverless taxis, became tangled up in Albany politics.
articles.snippet: How two seemingly unrelated policies, one to reduce car insurance costs, the other to allow driverless taxis, became tangled up in Albany politics.
articles.byline: By Benjamin Oreskes and Stefanos Chen

articles.title: Jobs Evaporated Unexpectedly, a Troubling Sign for U.S. Economy
articles.abstract: Employers shed 92,000 jobs in February and the unemployment rate rose to 4.4 percent.
articles.snippet: Employers shed 92,000 jobs in February and the unemployment rate rose to 4.4 percent.

articles.title: These New Englanders Really Don’t Want the Patriots to Win the Super Bowl
articles.abstract: When the New England Patriots take the field on Sunday, a collective sigh will rise from the willful few who live in the region but root for other teams.
articles.snippet: When the New England Patriots take the field on Sunday, a collective sigh will rise from the willful few who live in the region but root for other teams.

articles.title: ‘Gang Stuff’ and ‘Illicit Trysts’: How Epstein Sought Leverage With the Wealthy
articles.abstract: Jeffrey Epstein drafted letters to men like the retail billionaire Leslie Wexner in which he hinted at shared secrets.
articles.snippet: Jeffrey Epstein drafted letters to men like the retail billionaire Leslie Wexner in which he hinted at shared secrets.

articles.title: The Difference That ICE Watchers Are Making in Minnesota
articles.abstract: The monitoring of federal agents by activists has been at the heart of the opposition to the Trump administration’s raids. The tactic has a long history.
articles.snippet: The monitoring of federal agents by activists has been at the heart of the opposition to the Trump administration’s raids. The tactic has a long history.

articles.title: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez Steps Onto a Wider Stage
articles.abstract: The progressive lawmaker is taking a larger role in Democratic politics, supporting moderate candidates and helping drive the party’s economic message. Now she is planning a major trip abroad.
articles.snippet: The progressive lawmaker is taking a larger role in Democratic politics, supporting moderate candidates and helping drive the party’s economic message. Now she is planning a major trip abroad.
articles.byline: By Kellen Browning, Reid J. Epstein and Leo Dominguez and McKinnon de Kuyper
